team.detail: 262 total headcount (2 EP, 4 SM, 12 Mgr, 28 Sr, 48 Staff, 168 GDS) is reasonable for final substantive on a $416B revenue client. Heavy GDS leverage (64% of headcount) reflects execution-intensive nature of substantive testing. All role names are valid EY roles. No Specialist role included, which is acceptable since specialist_evaluation is handled in separate specialist workflows.

allocation.detail: Total hours: ~28,550 over 3 weeks. EP at 0.15 (governance review) within 0.05-0.25 range. SM at 0.45 reflects increased final-phase oversight. Manager at 0.80, Senior at 0.92, Staff at 0.95, GDS at 0.92 all reflect near-full-time execution work appropriate for final substantive crunch. 28,550 hours is ~14.7% of 194,000 total engagement hours — reasonable for the most labor-intensive testing phase.

description.detail: AS 2905 is 'Subsequent Discovery of Facts Existing at the Date of the Auditor's Report' (a post-report standard). The correct standard for subsequent events testing during fieldwork is AS 2801 'Subsequent Events'. Apple-specific claims are generally accurate: September 27 FY-end is correct for FY2025, services revenue bundled arrangement testing is relevant, and ~$48.5B net PP&E is within ~3% of actual FY2025 figure ($49.8B) — acceptable as an approximation.

requirements.detail: substantive_procedures and subsequent_events are both correct and required. However, the description explicitly mentions 'Final analytics' which maps to final_analytical_review — this requirement should be included. misstatement_accumulation could also apply but is more naturally housed in the adjustments workflow.

timing.detail: Weeks 17.5-20.5 (3 weeks) aligns with post-FY-end final substantive window for a September 27 fiscal year. Interim substantive ends at week 16.5, providing a 1-week gap before final substantive begins — reasonable for transitioning from interim to final. Duration is tight but feasible given the 262-person team. Inputs (InterimResults, SpecialistOutput) are available from prior workflows.

corrections.reason: AS 2905 is 'Subsequent Discovery of Facts Existing at the Date of the Auditor's Report' — a post-report remedial standard. The correct standard for subsequent events testing during the audit is AS 2801 'Subsequent Events'.

The description explicitly references 'Final analytics' as part of this workflow's scope. final_analytical_review is a valid requirement_id and should be mapped since the workflow encompasses this procedure.
